notes: Planning seeds for Module 4 (BB Turn Defense OOP). 24 scenarios across 6 turn categories (4 each). NOT loaded at runtime. Migration to production scheduled for v4.3.1+ after seed strategic review (v4.3.0A).

question.prompt: Flop As 8d 3h; turn 2c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Th 8h?

explanation.short: Mid pair on dry A-high after brick turn -- fold; range disadvantage compounds.
explanation.turnLogic: The 2c is a brick: no draws complete, no overcards arrive. BTN barrel range narrows to A-x value + a few bluffs. Mid pair is below threshold.
explanation.rangeContext: After BB called the flop and BTN bets again, BTN is signaling stronger A-x or strong bluffs with backdoors that picked up. Brick 2c removes the bluffs that needed equity.
explanation.handLogic: Th8h has middle pair only; no flush draw equity (heart BDFD was alive on flop, 2c kills it).
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding is correct vs typical small barrel; calling vs over-bluffy villain is acceptable as exploit.
explanation.commonMistake: Stationing mid pair on bricked turns vs BTN A-high range bleeds chips.
explanation.takeaway: Mid pair bricked turn on range-disadvantaged board = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Brick turn after dry A-high flop: mid pair fold lesson. Tests recognition that brick turns favor BTN barrel range narrowing toward stronger A-x.

scenarios.blockerNote: Q kicker beats 80% of villain TPx (AT/AJ/A9 etc.); A blocker reduces villain AA value combos.

question.prompt: Flop As 8d 3h; turn 2c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ad Qd?

explanation.short: TPGK on dry A-high brick turn -- call to bluff-catch barrel range.
explanation.turnLogic: 2c brick keeps ranges static. BTN barrel still contains AK/AQ/AJ value + some semi-bluff air; AQ beats most of the air bucket.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flatted preflop with AQ; flop call was easy. Turn call captures villain bluffs without bloating vs AK.
explanation.handLogic: AdQd has TPGK + backdoor diamond + A blocker on AA. Strong continue.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves villain bluffs; raising folds them out and isolates AK.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding TPGK on dry brick turns over-folds vs villain bluff frequency.
explanation.takeaway: TPGK on dry brick = call (do not fold and do not raise).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TPGK call lesson on brick turn. Distinct from M3 because BB has now committed an extra street; the call equity calculus differs from flop bluff-catch.

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero blocks 88 cooler combos (only one 8 left in deck).

question.prompt: Flop As 8d 3h; turn 2c. BB calls with 8c 8h vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Set of 8s on dry A-high brick turn -- slowplay to keep villain bluff range alive.
explanation.turnLogic: 2c brick means AA/AK/AQ/AJ value range stays largely the same; BTN-air-with-equity dies. Slowplay collects barrel chips.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flat-called the flop with 88 (set). On brick turn, value check-raise folds out the bluffs that pay off river barrels.
explanation.handLogic: Set of 8s loses only to AA (improbable here as raise pre would be common); has near-100% equity vs barrel range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling lets BTN keep firing river. Raising small is acceptable to charge AK/AQ but loses river barrel value.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising flopped set on brick turns folds out the wide bluff bucket.
explanation.takeaway: Set on brick turn = slowplay call by default; small raise acceptable.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Reason_choice testing slowplay vs raise distinction on brick turn. Tests recognition that brick turns reduce villain equity bluff range -- slowplay > raise.

question.prompt: Flop As 8d 3h; turn 2c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Js Th?

explanation.short: JT no draw after brick turn -- fold; no equity, no path forward.
explanation.turnLogic: 2c brick kills any backdoor draw JT had on the flop (no spade/club shared). Hero is now drawing dead vs Ax value.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop float with JT was thin; brick turn turns it into pure giveaway.
explanation.handLogic: JsTh has no pair, no draw, no flush blocker; 6 overcard outs that often pair villain kicker.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding is automatic; calling commits chips with zero equity into A-x heavy range.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing turn floats with no equity is the most expensive postflop leak.
explanation.takeaway: Bricked float = fold (do not call to "see one more card").

scenarios.uniquenessNote: CRITICAL teaching: bricked float fold. Distinct lesson: turn brick removes the only equity (backdoor) the float had; calling is uniquely worst.

question.prompt: Flop 9d 8c 6h; turn Kc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 9c 9s?

explanation.short: Top set 9s on K-overcard turn -- raise for value AND protection.
explanation.turnLogic: Kc overcard SHIFTS range advantage to BTN (more K-x value combos), but top set still way ahead. Many turns next street kill action; raise now while villain has K-x to call.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped middle set; turn K turns it into still-strong but no longer top set. Villain now has Kx + KK / TT / JJ / QQ / sets / straights more weighted.
explanation.handLogic: 99 makes set on 9-8-6; Kc only beats us in Kx hands which we mostly want to charge. Loses only to KK, sets above (rare), straight (T7, 75 rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges Kx; big raise also viable on dynamic turns.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying flopped set on overcard turn surrenders value vs Kx that pays off raise.
explanation.takeaway: Top set on overcard turn = raise (protection + value); call also fine.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Overcard turn protection-raise lesson with set. Tests that overcard turn shifts range but does not eliminate top-set value -- raise > slowplay because Kx now exists to charge.

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero made nut straight 6-7-8-9-T on the flop. Tc adds bonus club FD redraw on river clubs.

question.prompt: Flop 9d 8c 6h; turn Kc. BB check-raises small with Tc 7c vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Made nut straight on K-overcard turn -- value check-raise.
explanation.turnLogic: Kc turn does not change Tc7c equity -- hero already made the nut straight 6-7-8-9-T on the flop. Villain barrel range heavy in Kx pair-types is dominated; raising extracts value.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with T7s landed the nut straight on flop; Kc adds Kx hands to villain barrel range, all of which are dominated by hero straight.
explanation.handLogic: Tc7c has the nut straight (6-7-8-9-T) plus a club FD redraw (4 clubs vs 1 needed on river).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise extracts value from Kx and protects against runout pairing the board to give villain a higher straight or boat draw.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as semi-bluff misses that hero ALREADY has the made nut straight; raise mechanism is value, not fold equity.
explanation.takeaway: Made nut straight on overcard turn = value check-raise vs Kx-heavy barrel range.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Reason_choice testing value_check_raise_turn on a made nut straight vs other raise reasons. Tests that nut made hand drives value raise, not bluff/protection/blocker (note: T7 makes 6-7-8-9-T straight already on the flop).

question.prompt: Flop 9d 8c 6h; turn Kc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 9h 7h?

explanation.short: Top pair 9 weak kicker on K-overcard turn -- fold; range advantage shifted hard.
explanation.turnLogic: Kc overcard means BTN now has every K-x in barrel range, including KQ/KJ/AK; hero 9-pair drops below the threshold.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with mid pair + gutshot was fine; turn K shifts range and kills gutshot equity (need T or 5 for straight, K does not help).
explanation.handLogic: 9h7h has top pair 9 with 7 kicker + gutshot. K barrel turns this into clear underdog vs barrel range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs into K-x heavy range.
explanation.commonMistake: Stationing top-pair-weak-kicker on overcard turns is the prototypical M4 leak.
explanation.takeaway: Top pair on overcard turn that brings villain range advantage = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Overcard turn board-change-fold lesson. Distinct from brick-turn fold because the trigger is the K turn shifting villain range rather than equity simply not existing.

question.prompt: Flop 9d 8c 6h; turn Kc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ad Js?

explanation.short: AJ no pair no draw on K-overcard turn -- fold; six overcards are mostly dominated.
explanation.turnLogic: Kc turn does not help AdJs at all -- hero misses every flop card and has no straight or flush draw. Villain barrel range is range-advantaged + nut-advantaged on this runout.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AdJs on 9d 8c 6h was already thin (overcards only, no draw). Turn K shifts range hard to villain Kx.
explanation.handLogic: AdJs has no pair, no draw. Six overcard outs (3 aces + 3 jacks) but villain Kx range dominates Jx and many Ax pair outs lose to better Kings.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action and saves chips; raising as a bluff has near-zero fold equity vs Kx-anchored barrel range.
explanation.commonMistake: Calling AJ on overcard turns hoping to hit one of six outs ignores the mostly-dominated reality and burns chips.
explanation.takeaway: Naked overcards with no draw on overcard turn = fold; raise with no fold equity is a punt.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: CRITICAL teaching: naked overcards + no draw fold on overcard turn. Distinct from 2.3 (top-pair-weak-kicker fold) because here hero has zero made-hand value and overcard outs are dominated; tests that overcard equity alone does not rescue range disadvantage.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 6s 5s?

explanation.short: Made low flush on flush-completing turn -- call; raising bloats vs higher flushes.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; BTN barrel range now contains AsX/QsX higher flushes + air with no spade.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped low flush draw + small straight draw, called flop. Turn 2s makes flush; raising commits vs Ax-of-spades.
explanation.handLogic: 6s5s has 6-high flush + small backdoor straight (3-4-5-6-7 still alive on river).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air-bluffs without bloating vs nut flush. Small raise occasionally to charge worse flushes.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising any flush on monotone turns commits OOP into nut-flush range.
explanation.takeaway: Made low flush on flush-complete turn = call (raise small as alt; never big).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Made low flush bluff-catch lesson on flush-complete turn. Tests not over-bloating with non-nut flush vs villain Ax-of-suit.

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-flush card; blocks every Ax-of-spades flush combo in BTN range.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Kd?

explanation.short: TPTK with nut-flush blocker on flush-complete turn -- call; nut blocker turns this into bluff-catcher.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; As blocks nut-flush combos and reduces villain barrel value range.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flat with AK pre, flop call with TPTK was easy. Turn flush threat looks scary, but As blocker = villain has no nut flush.
explanation.handLogic: AsKd has TPTK + As nut FD blocker + 2nd-nut redraw potential.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air; raising acceptable to charge K-flush combos but bloats OOP.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding TPTK on flush-complete turns when holding the As blocker over-folds vs villain barrel range.
explanation.takeaway: TPTK + nut-flush blocker on flush-complete turn = call (do not fold).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TPTK + nut blocker bluff-catch lesson on flush-complete turn. Tests recognition that nut blocker turns scary turn into call spot.

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-spade blocker; hero removes every Ax-spade nut-flush combo from villain barrel range. No made hand and no draw -- pure blocker bluff.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BB check-raises small with As Jd vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Nut spade blocker on flush-complete turn -- blocker check-raise (advanced; solver mixes).
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; As is the nut spade blocker, removing every villain Ax-spade nut-flush combo and turning hero into a leveraged blocker bluff candidate.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AsJd was natural (broadway high cards, A-blocker presence). Turn 2s makes raising a pure blocker bluff line vs villain barrel range.
explanation.handLogic: AsJd has no made hand and no draw; As alone removes nut-spade combos from villain range. Pure blocker bluff.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise size leverages the nut blocker; large raise risks too much when called by lower flushes.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as semi-bluff (no draw) or value (no made hand) misses that the raise mechanism is purely the As blocker.
explanation.takeaway: Nut spade blocker on flush-complete turn = blocker check-raise (advanced; mix-frequency line).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Reason_choice testing blocker_check_raise_turn vs other raise/fold reasons. Tests that pure nut-spade blocker (no draw, no pair) drives raise reason = blocker, not semi-bluff/value/protection. Distinct from 3.2 because hero has zero made-hand component, making blocker the only raise mechanism.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Tc 9c?

explanation.short: No spade no draw on flush-complete turn -- fold; zero flush equity and dominated overcards.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; without a spade, hero has no flush threat and faces a polarized barrel range (flushes + air-with-no-spade).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with T9 on K-high two-tone was already thin (gutshot to J for QJ-x straight). Turn 2s kills the gutshot.
explanation.handLogic: Tc9c has no spade, no pair, no current draw. The straight gutshot (need J for K-Q-J-T-9 wait there is no Q on board, so no straight available). Pure giveup.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling on flush turn with zero flush equity is uniquely worst.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing turn with no spade on flush-completing turns is the prototypical leak.
explanation.takeaway: No spade + no draw on flush turn = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: CRITICAL teaching: no-spade fold on flush-complete turn. Distinct lesson: turn polarization eliminates equity for hands without flush card.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 9c 8h?

explanation.short: 8-9-T-J-Q straight on straight-complete turn -- raise for value.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes straight for 98 / KT / 89; BB flop call range with OESD + flush draws hits this turn hard. Range advantage swings to BB.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 98 (open-ender + flush draw) was strong; turn J makes straight. Villain barrel range still has Q-x / overpairs / TPTK that pay off raise.
explanation.handLogic: 98 makes 8-9-T-J-Q straight; only KT/T9 (not possible with 9 in hand) and KQ-flushes beat us (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise gets called by Q-x and overpairs; big raise also defensible vs nut-flush combos.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying turned straight on straight-complete turn surrenders huge value vs villain barrel range.
explanation.takeaway: Made straight on straight-complete turn = raise for value.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Made straight value-raise on straight-complete turn. Distinct from set-on-overcard lesson because hero hits the turn while villain range does not improve.

scenarios.blockerNote: K blocks K-high straight combos KT/K9; hero card is part of multiple straight structures.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Kh Qh?

explanation.short: TPGK + gutshot to K-Q-J-T-A on straight-complete turn -- call.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes some straights but BB Q-x is still strong. Hero KhQh has top pair (Q) + redraw to nut straight (need 9 for K-Q-J-T-9 wait 9-T-J-Q-K = open-ender now? Need 9 makes 9-T-J-Q-K, need A makes T-J-Q-K-A. Both straight outs!).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KQ on QT6 was natural (TPGK + backdoor). Turn J adds gutshot/OE redraw.
explanation.handLogic: KhQh has TPGK + redraw to nut straight (T-J-Q-K-A or 9-T-J-Q-K).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air-bluffs + leaves redraw alive. Small raise also acceptable.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding TPGK + redraw vs straight-complete turn over-folds vs villain barrel.
explanation.takeaway: TPGK + nut redraw on straight turn = call.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TPGK + nut-redraw call lesson on straight-complete turn. Tests recognition that own pair + redraw to higher straight beats villain weaker top pair barrels.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BB calls with Tc 9d vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: T9 with double-gutshot + middle pair on straight-complete turn -- call to realize equity.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes some straights but T9 still has equity: pair of T + double-gutshot to 8 (8-9-T-J-Q) and K (9-T-J-Q-K).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with mid pair + gutshot was thin; turn J adds OE redraw potential.
explanation.handLogic: Tc9d has middle pair (T) + 8 outs to straight (need 8 for 8-9-T-J-Q or K for 9-T-J-Q-K).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes the multi-source equity at minimum cost. Raising too thin OOP without made hand stronger.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding the double-gutshot + pair under-defends vs villain barrel range that has air.
explanation.takeaway: Pair + double-gutshot on straight-complete turn = call; raise too thin.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Reason_choice testing equity_realization_turn_call vs bluff_catch_turn vs pot_odds_turn_call. Tests learner can distinguish "call to realize equity" from pure bluff-catch.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 5h 4d?

explanation.short: 54 no pair no draw on straight-complete turn -- fold; bottom-of-flop range has no equity post-J.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes higher straights for villain barrel range (KT/89 etc.); 54 had backdoor potential on flop, killed by J.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 54 on Q-T-6 two-tone was thin (gutshot to 7 for 4-5-6-7-8 only); turn J kills it.
explanation.handLogic: 5h4d has no pair, no draw, no blocker; backdoor that died on the J.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling commits with zero equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing weak floats through straight-completing turns is uniquely worst.
explanation.takeaway: Bricked low float on straight turn = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: CRITICAL teaching: bricked float on straight-complete turn. Distinct from 1.4 because the trigger here is a draw-completing turn that crushes BB low-end range, not a brick.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ah 3d?

explanation.short: Trip 3 paired turn = full house (3s full of 8s) -- slowplay; bluff range alive.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h pairs the 3 making BB trips into full house. BTN barrel range now contains overpairs / Ax-air / 88 (rare boats).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with A3o (likely thin float pre but consistent) -- turn 3 makes full house.
explanation.handLogic: Ah3d has full house 3s over 8s; only 88 (improbable) beats. Loses nothing on river.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves villain bluffs for river barrel; raising folds out air.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising boat on paired-paired turns folds bluffs that would barrel river.
explanation.takeaway: Full house on board-pairing turn = slowplay (do not raise).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Full house slowplay on double-paired turn. Distinct lesson: turn pair makes monster, but villain range now has fewer drawing hands and many bluffs that would barrel river.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 5h 5d?

explanation.short: Pocket 5s on double-paired turn -- bluff-catch; pair beats air despite counterfeit risk.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h pairs board so hero plays 8-8-3-3 + 5 = two pair with 5 kicker. Counterfeited overpair to 3 but still beats every air-barrel.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 55 on 88-3 was bluff-catch territory; turn 3 keeps bluff catcher status (pair of 5 kicker + board two pair).
explanation.handLogic: 5h5d has pocket fives; final hand 8-8-3-3-5 beats all air, loses to 88 / overpairs / Ax with 3.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air; raising folds bluffs and isolates better hands.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding small pocket pair on counterfeit-paired turns over-folds vs villain bluff-heavy range.
explanation.takeaway: Counterfeited underpair on double-paired turn = call (do not fold to "scary turn").

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Counterfeited underpair bluff-catch on board-pair turn. Distinct lesson: turn counterfeit looks scary but pair still beats air.

scenarios.blockerNote: A and K block AA / KK overpair value combos.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BB calls with Ad Kc vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: AK no pair on double-paired turn -- bluff-catch; A blocks villain top of range.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h double-pairs board; villain barrel range has overpairs + air. AK with Ace blocker beats all air.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AK on 88-3 was either thin float or blocker bet candidate. Turn 3 keeps bluff catch status.
explanation.handLogic: AdKc has no pair, no draw, but A blocks AA top-of-range and 6 overcard outs (river A or K).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures bluffs; raising folds them out; folding under-defends.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as range_disadvantage_turn_fold misses the A-blocker bluff-catch line.
explanation.takeaway: AK on counterfeit-paired turn = bluff-catch (call), not fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Reason_choice testing bluff_catch_turn vs range_disadvantage_turn_fold. Tests learner recognizes A-blocker turns no-pair into bluff-catcher.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Qh Jh?

explanation.short: QJ no pair no draw on double-paired turn -- fold; no equity even on counterfeit board.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h double-pairs board. QJ has 6 overcard outs but pair of Q or J still loses to overpairs / Ax with 3 / 88.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with QJ on 88-3 was already thin (no pair, no draw, no relevant blocker); turn 3 keeps the situation bad.
explanation.handLogic: QhJh has no pair, no draw, no blocker. 6 pair outs are dominated.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Floating QJ "because two overcards" on paired boards leaks chips.
explanation.takeaway: Naked QJ on paired board after turn pair = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: CRITICAL teaching: naked QJ fold on counterfeit-paired turn. Distinct lesson: even paired boards have a defense floor; QJ no-blocker no-draw is below it.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Tc Td?

explanation.short: Top set T on draw-intensifier turn -- raise for protection AND value.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h adds 7 + 8 to straight-completing rivers (5-6-7-8-9 or 6-7-8-9-T). Many rivers kill action; raise now charges draws.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped top set; turn 6 adds new straight draws to villain range (78s) and BB needs to charge.
explanation.handLogic: TcTd makes top set; loses only to 99/55 sets and J-87 / 89 / 7-8 straights (rare flop call combos).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges OE/gutshots without folding K-x air entirely; big raise also defensible.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top set on draw-heavy turns lets villain realize equity for free.
explanation.takeaway: Top set on draw-intensifier turn = raise for protection.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Top set protection-raise on draw-intensifier turn. Distinct from 2.1 because the trigger is new draws, not range shift; protection > slowplay.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 8c 7c?

explanation.short: Made straight 6-7-8-9-T on draw-intensifier turn -- raise for value.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h completes straight for 87/89; BB flop call with 87 OE was strong, turn 6 makes hand.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped strong combo (OE + flush draw); turn 6 makes nut straight on a dynamic board.
explanation.handLogic: 87 makes 6-7-8-9-T straight; only 8-7 (us) and J-x straight (J7-9 = 7-8-9-T-J needs J) compete (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges Tx + overpairs + draws; big raise defensible vs higher draws.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying turned straight on draw-heavy turn lets villain realize equity.
explanation.takeaway: Made straight on draw-intensifier turn = raise for value.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Made-straight value-raise on draw-intensifier turn. Distinct from 4.1 because turn brings additional draws to villain range alongside straight completion.

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-flush blocker on spades.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As 6s?

explanation.short: Nut FD + pair of 6 + Ace overcard on draw-intensifier turn -- call to realize multi-source equity.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h pairs hero turn card; nut FD still alive (4 spades visible: Ts, 9s, As, 6s); plus gutshot to 7 (5-6-7-8-9).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped nut FD + Ace overcard; turn 6 adds pair + new straight draws.
explanation.handLogic: As6s has nut FD (1 more spade for nut flush) + pair of 6 + 3 overcard outs + nut blocker.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes ~50% equity cheaply. Raising too thin OOP given turn deepens pot more than flop.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding combo equity OOP under-defends vs villain barrel.
explanation.takeaway: Combo draw + pair on dynamic turn = call to realize equity.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Combo-draw equity_realization_turn_call lesson on draw-intensifier turn. Tests that combo equity sources -> call > raise on deeper-pot turn street.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BB folds with Kh Qd vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: KQ no spade no pair no draw on draw-intensifier turn -- fold; range disadvantage with no equity.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h adds new draws to villain range while BB KQ has no spade for FD and gutshot to J still alive but dominated.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KQ on T-9-5 two-tone was thin (gutshot to J). Turn 6 keeps gutshot but adds villain draws + reduces relative equity.
explanation.handLogic: KhQd has gutshot to J (need J for K-Q-J-T-9? need K-Q-J-T- + 9 yes works) but pair outs dominated.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action cleanly; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as bluff_catch_turn misses that no pair / no FD / no blocker has no bluff-catch claim.
explanation.takeaway: KQ no equity no blocker on dynamic turn = range_disadvantage fold (not bluff-catch).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: CRITICAL reason_choice teaching: distinguish range_disadvantage_turn_fold from bluff_catch_turn / board_change_fold. Tests reasoning chain on dynamic turn fold.
